methods.description: This method describes the calculation flow of the models used to simulate the physical and chemical conditions in astrophysical environments. It involves solving for dust and gas temperatures, calculating local radiation fields, and determining chemical abundances and molecular excitation.

steps.description: The modeling process starts with assuming a density structure and the spectrum of the central source.
steps.searchable_summary: initialize model with density structure and central source spectrum

steps.description: A dust radiative transfer calculation then solves for the dust temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: perform dust radiative transfer calculation to determine dust temperature

steps.description: The local FUV and X-ray radiation is calculated at every point of the model as described in Appendix A.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate local FUV and X-ray radiation at each model point

steps.description: The gas temperature is obtained by equating the heating and cooling rates.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate gas temperature by equating heating and cooling rates

steps.description: The calculation of the gas temperature has to be performed simultaneously with the chemistry using a grid of chemical models.
steps.searchable_summary: perform simultaneous calculation of gas temperature and chemistry using chemical model grid

steps.description: The molecular excitation is calculated using the escape probability approach described in Appendix B.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate molecular excitation using escape probability approach

methods.method_name: Density Structure and Cavity Shape Modeling
methods.description: This method involves adopting a density structure from previous models and exploring variations in cavity shapes and other parameters to study their effects on chemical models.

steps.description: Adopt the density structure of the spherical model by van der Tak et al. (1999).
steps.searchable_summary: adopt density structure from van der Tak et al. (1999)
inputs: spherical model by van der Tak et al. (1999)

steps.description: Assume the cavity shape follows a power-law with index b.
steps.searchable_summary: define cavity shape with power-law index b

steps.description: Explore parameter range by varying the outflow opening angle α, the power-law index b, cavity density γ, and the bolometric luminosity of the protostar L_bol.
steps.searchable_summary: vary parameters α, b, γ, and L_bol to explore model variations
